description: The Honoured Society is a Calabrian 'ndrangheta criminal confederation active in Australia, founded around 1920 in Melbourne. It controls Italian-Australian organized crime along the East Coast, involved in racketeering, drug trafficking, and other illegal activities. The organization has been marked by internal power struggles and conflicts such as the Market Wars. It remains active with multiple 'ndrine (crime families) operating across Australia.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Honoured Society ... is a Calabrian 'ndrangheta criminal confederation, started in Melbourne and currently active in all of Australia.
country_links.context: The Honoured Society is active throughout Australia, with cells in Melbourne, Sydney, Perth, and other areas, and carries out organized crime activities including drug trafficking, extortion, and murder.

country_links.evidence_quote: the 'ndrangheta were tied to the importation of 15 million ecstasy pills to Melbourne, at the time the world's largest ecstasy haul. The pills were hidden in a container-load of tomato cans from Calabria.
country_links.context: The organization sources drugs from Italy, as evidenced by the importation of ecstasy pills hidden in tomato cans from Calabria.
